title: sanitize.rs rebuilds HashSets on every call
description:
src/sanitize.rs:6-23, 47-49 — sanitize_html() and html_to_text() create new HashSet instances on every invocation:

    let tags: HashSet<&str> = ["p", "br", ...].into_iter().collect();
    let a_attrs: HashSet<&str> = ["href"].into_iter().collect();

And html_to_text:

    Builder::new().tags(std::collections::HashSet::new()).clean(html).to_string()

These are called in hot paths (every post creation, every feed entry, every webhook, every outbox page render). The tag and attribute sets are static constants. Use LazyLock<HashSet<&str>> or a once-built Builder to avoid per-call allocation.

Impact: Unnecessary per-call allocations. For a feed poll importing 100 entries, that is 200+ HashSet constructions.
close_reason: Duplicate of broadside-goj2.13 (same finding, same fix)
